string 2:
Wow... It feels like I just graduated high school, but I
was, like, really young then.
...Hm? Look at this class photo.
I never noticed this before, but he's in the picture, too.
{next}Who're you talking about?
{next}You just now noticed?
{next}Okay, I confess. I'm in there, too.

Yeah, an old classmate of mine. He definitely had his
quirks, so to speak.

I almost never look at old photos. It reminds me of the
people I lost, like you.
Quit playing around! I'm being serious!

You see, he asked me for advice on finding his missing
parents, but I couldn't help him.

We met in Shinjuku Park, so I thought he had something heavy
to talk about. Ah, memories.

If we could go back in time, I may be able to help him, what
with you there and all.

We actually didn't get along very well. His personality
could be somewhat... difficult.

Still, I've always regretted not being able to help him find
his parents.

If I could've had your help eight years ago, I might've been
able to assist him.

Wow, what a coincidence! I was just about to give you a
call.

Care to help with something I'm having a bit of trouble
with?

So you're the one that Hiroko is always turning to for
assistance.
{next}Probably, yeah.
{end}You got the wrong person.

Meet my former classmate, Kinshiro Eriito. He came to me on
a slightly complicated issue.
What is your name? I shall dedicate it to memory.
You should be honored to be assisting a genius like me.

As you can see, he's a bit eccentric, but he's literally
right about being a genius.

Naturally, he always had the highest test scores. He'd been
acing university entrance exams since elementary school.

This bears repeating, but his personality can be a bit
weird. He's a genius at that, too.
{next}Balance in any and all things.
{next}Is this, like, his default personality?
{next}So he's even more quirky than you?

You can say that again. I guess everything has its
trade-offs, even being a genius.

I'd say so. Being arrogant and rude is pretty much his
standard operating procedure!

H-Hey, I'm NOT quirky! Is that how you see me?! I gotta say,
I'm shocked to hear that!
Anyway, he's asked for help finding his missing parents.

It's complicated, but it seems his parents provided their
DNA, yet never actually met.

I do not even know what my parents look like. That is why I
wish to meet them.

I was originally born in a place called Genius Labs, and I
live there to this day.

They gave me the perfect genetic combination. Basically, I
was a genius from birth.
{next}A so-called designer baby?
{next}What do you know about your parents?
{next}What does all that mean?

I am surprised you know that term. You must be quite
knowledgeable about the subject.

Most information about my genetic parents has been kept from
me.

I thought I sufficiently simplified my explanation, but you
still seem confused.

I am an advanced lifeform that has taken the next step in
human evolution.

Compared to me, people such as you are mere apes. Maybe
chimpanzees, if I'm generous.

Um... Talk about yourself all you want, but you shouldn't
talk about others like that.

And why not? I am merely stating what has proven to be an
objective fact.

I convinced the institute to let me attend high school so I
could study those of low IQ.

Yeah, you did transfer at a rather odd time. And it's true
our school wasn't that great.

Indeed. My purpose was to see how inferior beings lived in
such imperfect conditions.

However, it was nice to enjoy a brief respite from always
being confined to my studies.

It was there that I met someone working with a detective
agency and conducting interviews.
You mean me...

I wished to know what my parents were like and what they had
possibly accomplished.

Plus, I wanted a mother and father with whom I could have a
discussion on my own level.

Yet, I know not the ways of this simplistic world, so I need
help finding my parents.

I don't like the way you call us simplistic, but I get you
wanting to know your parents.

Genius Labs... We know someone who's probably familiar with
that research institute, right?
{next}Somone with public safety?
{next}Dr. Simmons?
{next}Yeah... me!

Hmm, I'm not so sure. Do you know anyone investigating that
place?
Doctor...? Oh, you mean that oddball you told me about?

C'mon. I saw your blank expression when he mentioned Genius
Labs. You don't have a clue.

I haven't met this Dr. Simmons yet, but maybe she would know
something.

If you think this person can help, I ask that you take me to
her without delay.

Now that I think about it... I did see a lady in a lab coat
a bit ago.

She had blonde hair you couldn't miss and was headed toward
Subroad. Wanna go see?
...Can I help you with something?

We're looking for Dr. Simmons. Seen anyone in a lab coat
around here?
Dr. Simmons was just here a little while ago.
She left once she finished her business.
Did she mention where she was headed...?

I probably shouldn't divulge our activities, but Dr. Simmons
does appreciate your help...

She also said she hoped to speak to you again, so I'll make
an exception. Check Nishi-Shinjuku, the business district.
Thanks! That's a big help!

Hmm. A most talented specimen indeed. Yet she still pales in
comparison to me...

S-Sorry about that! C'mon. Let's get going! I said, let's
go, already!
Oh, hey there! You came at just the perfect time.
Why are all these monsters attacking you?!
In my foolishness, I got too close.
Well, you seem pretty calm despite being attacked...

I'm half terrified... and half overjoyed at all the
firsthand data I'm collecting...
D-Definitely as weird as I'd heard. We gotta help her...!
Thanks for the help. You have my gratitude.
I don't like your chances if we hadn't shown up...

Well, sometimes, luck is an important part of being a
genius.
Wait. So you're a genius, too, are you?
...Hm? Who's this boy?
Well, it's a long story. You see...
...Oh. I get it now.

I do know about Genius Labs. However, I don't see much point
in contacting them directly.
So you can't help us, either...
I didn't say that. I might know of a way.

There must be some data on his parents, and it's probably
highly confidential.

They won't just give it to you, so you'll need to hack into
their system to access it.

I thought about that, but the cybersecurity at Genius Labs
is top-notch.

I could not break through their firewall... I think my
computer was not powerful enough.
Well, you don't need to worry about computers.
My machine should be enough to hack into their system.
You're willing to help us...?

We at public safety have our eyes on Genius Labs. There's
talk they're up to illegal genetic modifications.

And this boy... can also be taken to be a victim of that.
So, I'll lend a hand, also as thanks for just now.
...Yes!

If I take an interest in something, it doesn't take me long
to accomplish it.
Just wait there a moment.
Oh... I see... How fascinating...
Well, well... You don't say...?
{next}You found the data?
{next}Is something wrong?
{next}Did you fail to hack into the system?
Yes, there's definitely data here, but...

Well, it's the kind of stuff even I'd think twice about
sharing.

Of course not. It's because I WAS successful that I'm
somewhat hesitant at the moment...
What are you waiting for? Tell me what you found.

Who are my parents? Were they university professors?
Politicians? Entrepreneurs?
No, they were none of those things.
In fact, they weren't even human.
Wh-What do you mean?!

Take a look for yourself. It's a picture of Kinshiro's
parents.
They're... apes?

Chimpanzees, to be precise. I read his official file.
There's no doubt about it.

The parents who provided your genetic material were
chimpanzees.

Hahaha... Chimpanzees? How am I supposed to be able to talk
to them now...?
Wow, Kinshiro... I don't know what to say...

This is also difficult for me to tell you, but you deserve
to know the truth.

You're a verifiable genius now, but, in time, you'll end up
becoming like your parents.
In other words, you'll slowly begin to lose your humanity.

I...? I am going to be an ape? I will not even be able to
speak...!
I...! I cannot...!
Kinshiro!
He ran off...

Let him go. That's a lot for a person to take in all at
once.
Hey, how long does he have before he turns into an ape?

While it's only conjecture at this point, I'd say it'll
start in about five to six years.

And they ran a simulation that predicts he'll be pretty much
full-on ape in eight years.

His personality is quite abrasive, and, to be honest, I'm
not really all that fond of him.

But I understand wanting a mom and dad, and being able to
have conversations with them.
I've had experience seeing a friend lose a parent...

Forcing evolution in apes to create man-made geniuses? Not a
shred of beauty in it...
I'm worried about Kinshiro...

He's due to turn into an ape in eight years... What will
become of him?

Well, let's see. Humans descended from apes, and then a
genius was created from them...

In short, I have absolutely no idea what'll become of him in
eight years.

But if I had to guess, I don't think we'll be seeing him
around here anymore.

Eight years until he becomes a full-fledged ape... I wonder
what Kinshiro will be like?
...Huh? Oh, it's you...! Do you remember me?

My name is Kinshiro Eriito. You helped me search for my
parents eight years ago.
{next}You're still human?
{next}What're you up to these days?
{next}Are you feeling all right?

Ah, you were worried about me. As you can see, I am still
very much human.

My lab is near here, so I was just taking a break and
decided to go for a walk.
Oh, yes. I'm fine for the most part.

I admit, it took me some time eight years ago to accept that
my parents were apes.

However, once I accepted my new reality, I began searching
for a way to remain human.

As you can see, the research into preventing my
transformation is going well so far.
{next}It's like you're a different person.
{next}Are there any side effects?
{next}Maybe it's just a coincidence?

Yes, I suppose so. I imagine I used to be quite arrogant
back then.

Nothing that affects my life, per se. Maybe just that I am
unable to stop my research?
...What? Oh, I see. I'm not angry. In fact, I...

Many people have aided my research, which has helped me
realize the importance of others.

Oh, I managed to meet my parents, too. When I called them
"Mom and Dad," they scratched me!

At last, I can thank you for your help. Please take this as
a token of my gratitude.
